skill.name: defi-protocol-templates
skill.description: Implement DeFi protocols with production-ready templates for staking, AMMs, governance, and lending systems. Use when building decentralized finance applications or smart contract protocols.
skill.summary: Production-ready Solidity templates for staking, AMMs, governance tokens, lending protocols, and flash loans.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer produced false positives by misidentifying Solidity smart contract code as malicious scripts. All flagged patterns (require statements, C2 keywords, crypto algorithms) are standard DeFi/smart contract constructs. The skill contains educational Solidity templates only with no code execution, network access, or filesystem operations. Safe for publication.

content.user_title: Build DeFi Protocols with Production Templates
content.value_statement: Building DeFi applications requires secure, battle-tested smart contract patterns. This skill provides production-ready Solidity templates for staking, AMMs, governance, lending, and flash loans.
actual_capabilities: Generate staking contracts with reward distribution mechanisms
Create AMM (Automated Market Maker) implementations with liquidity pools
Build governance token systems with voting functionality
Implement flash loan providers and receivers
Provide lending and borrowing protocol templates
limitations: Templates require security audit before production deployment
Does not execute or deploy contracts - generates code only
Gas optimization may require chain-specific adjustments
Not a substitute for professional smart contract auditing

use_cases.title: DeFi Startup MVP
use_cases.description: Quickly prototype a staking platform or AMM for testing and investor demos

use_cases.title: Educational Learning
use_cases.description: Study production-ready patterns for staking, governance, and liquidity pools

use_cases.title: Protocol Integration
use_cases.description: Add staking or governance features to existing token projects

prompt_templates.title: Basic Staking Contract
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a staking contract where users can stake ERC20 tokens and earn rewards over time. Include stake, withdraw, and claim reward functions.

prompt_templates.title: AMM with Liquidity Pools
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an automated market maker contract with addLiquidity, removeLiquidity, and swap functions. Include constant product formula and 0.3% fee.

prompt_templates.title: Governance Token System
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a governance token with voting capabilities and a governor contract for proposal creation, voting, and execution.

prompt_templates.title: Flash Loan Provider
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a flash loan contract with fee mechanism and a receiver contract that executes arbitrage logic within the callback.

output_examples.input: Create a staking rewards contract for my ERC20 token
output_examples.output: Generates a complete StakingRewards contract with stake(), withdraw(), getReward(), and exit() functions, using OpenZeppelin's ReentrancyGuard for security.

output_examples.input: Build an AMM for token swaps
output_examples.output: Provides a SimpleAMM contract with liquidity management, constant product pricing, and swap functionality with fee collection.

best_practices: Always use OpenZeppelin libraries for standard ERC20 and security patterns
Implement reentrancy guards on all state-changing functions
Test thoroughly with unit tests and consider professional audits before deployment
anti_patterns: Deploying unaudited contracts to mainnet with real value
Using deprecated Solidity versions or unsafe patterns
Ignoring gas optimization for frequently-called functions

faq.question: Are these templates production-ready?
faq.answer: Templates follow best practices but require security audit and testing before production deployment with real funds.

faq.question: Which Solidity version should I use?
faq.answer: Templates use Solidity ^0.8.0 which includes built-in overflow protection. Adjust based on your project requirements.

faq.question: Do I need OpenZeppelin contracts?
faq.answer: Yes, install OpenZeppelin contracts via npm: npm install @openzeppelin/contracts for the imports to work.

faq.question: Can I modify these templates?
faq.answer: Yes, templates are MIT licensed. Customize them for your specific use case and token economics.

faq.question: What testing framework should I use?
faq.answer: Common choices include Hardhat with Waffle, Foundry for gas optimization, or Truffle for comprehensive testing.

faq.question: How do I add upgradability to these contracts?
faq.answer: Consider OpenZeppelin Upgradeable contracts and proxy patterns. This adds complexity and should be evaluated carefully.
